scripts._comment:compress: LOSSLESS pipeline. Skips `simplify` (drops triangles → visible detail loss) and `flatten`/`join` (rename meshes → breaks `classifyTrackMesh()`'s material lookup → asphalt/kerb/wall textures fall through to grey). Just dedup → webp (for any embedded textures) → re-Draco at MAX quantization (16-bit positions, 12-bit normals, 14-bit UVs, 16-bit colours). The original GLB was Draco-encoded with looser quantization defaults; re-encoding at higher quality + dedup of duplicate accessors gives a real reduction with zero geometric loss.
scripts.compress:track: gltf-transform dedup public/assets/models/track.glb /tmp/.gltf-step1.glb && gltf-transform webp /tmp/.gltf-step1.glb /tmp/.gltf-step2.glb && gltf-transform draco /tmp/.gltf-step2.glb public/assets/models/track.glb --quantize-position 16 --quantize-normal 12 --quantize-texcoord 14 --quantize-color 16 && rm /tmp/.gltf-step1.glb /tmp/.gltf-step2.glb
scripts.compress:car: gltf-transform dedup public/assets/models/m3_e46_steerable.glb /tmp/.gltf-step1.glb && gltf-transform webp /tmp/.gltf-step1.glb /tmp/.gltf-step2.glb && gltf-transform draco /tmp/.gltf-step2.glb public/assets/models/m3_e46_steerable.glb --quantize-position 16 --quantize-normal 12 --quantize-texcoord 14 --quantize-color 16 && rm /tmp/.gltf-step1.glb /tmp/.gltf-step2.glb
scripts.compress:assets: npm run compress:track && npm run compress:car
